channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How ChatGPT is helping bend websites to my will
item.description: I’m a writer, not a programmer, so until recently a lot of the hype around ChatGPT’s abitilies as a coding tool went over my head. But then I realized generative AI’s programming powers can be helpful for more than just coders. It can also help anyone else dabble in code to get things done.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Nintendo of America president Doug Bowser seemingly reveals the Nintendo Switch 2 Welcome Tour price
item.description: It looks like Nintendo of America president Doug Bowser has confirmed the price of the Nintendo Switch 2 Welcome Tour.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 users are getting fixes for some longstanding bugs in 24H2 today, including the cure for a seriously annoying File Explorer glitch
item.description: Microsoft is finally resolving some persistent bugs in Windows 11 24H2 with its April update which should be imminent now.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Shopify CEO Emphasizes Importance of AI in Daily Tasks
item.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke stresses the importance of using AI effectively in daily tasks, making it a fundamental expectation for employees.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX tourists return to Earth after a private trip that orbited the poles
item.description: Four space tourists who orbited the north and south poles returned to Earth on Friday, splashing down in the Pacific to end their privately funded polar tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How to watch Celebrity Big Brother 2025 online from anywhere – stream new series for free, channels, start times, housemates
item.description: 13 celebrities have entered the house and our guide explains how to watch Celebrity Big Brother 2025 online and stream episodes live and on demand for free.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Does Microsoft know something we don't? Tech giant cools down on AI data center investment as another report claims company pullbacks
item.description: A new report claims Microsoft has stepped away from even more data center projects worth billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: De ChatGPT a Chief AI Officer: el cambio en la era de los agentes digitales
item.description: Andrés Pazos, con una visión estratégica y pedagógica, abordó los principales retos y oportunidades que plantea la IA en múltiples sectores, desde la salud hasta el marketing, sin evitar asuntos espinosos como su elevado gasto energético o el miedo a utilizarla por el riesgo de infringir los derecho
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Silicon Valley isn’t pushing back on Trump’s immigration crackdown. It should be
item.description: President Donald Trump has just expanded the social media surveillance system for immigrants. Starting in May, the United States will begin collecting the social media handles of all permanent resident applicants.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoubleClickjacking hack turns double-clicks into account takeovers
item.description: Double-clicking is something we all do, often without giving it a second thought. But it could be giving hackers permission to do something dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech hiring dropped by 29K in March, even as US job growth remained solid
item.description: The tech market lost 29,000 jobs in March, even as the unemployment rate for tech-related jobs slipped 0.1% to 3.1%, according to an analysis of US Labor Department data released today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Cyber Call to Arms
item.description: China's growing cyber capabilities pose a significant threat to the United States, compromising critical infrastructure and networks with devastating consequences.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to remotely reset your router: Steps for easy troubleshooting
item.description: You can reset your internet router remotely if you're not home when your power goes out. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how this can be done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: From ensuring the quality of data to fostering a culture of experimentation and adaptability, leaders must actively steer AI’s integration into operations. The post Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft urges Office users to upgrade to 365
item.description: Microsoft is urging Office 2016 and 2019 customers to upgrade to Microsoft 365 before support ends Oct. 14, but analysts said viable alternatives are available outside Microsoft’s walled garden.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple feels gravity as the Trump tariff hammer falls
item.description: Apple’s leadership is no doubt scrambling to identify a silver lining (if there is one) as the storm of US President Donald J. Trump’s punishing range of global tariffs rains heavily across the company’s supply chain.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Relic simplifies Kubernetes performance monitoring
item.description: New Relic this week announced a new capability in its Intelligent Observability Platform that the company says will enable engineering teams to deploy an eBPF-powered agent to automatically discover and monitor all applications running on Kubernetes clusters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IBM Cloud speeds AI workloads with Intel Gaudi 3 accelerators
item.description: IBM Cloud is broadening its AI technology services with Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators now available to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CMMC review, multiuse SCIFs on radar of DoD acquisition nominee
item.description: Michael Duffey, the nominee to be USD for acquisition and sustainment, told lawmakers he's sensitive to CMMC compliance challenges for small businesses. The post CMMC review, multiuse SCIFs on radar of DoD acquisition nominee first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
